label: hard
description: Adversarial temporal queries — sparse documentation, contested dates, temporal layering, causal ambiguity. Designed so even 2030 frontier models rarely exceed 0.95

tasks.query: The Antikythera mechanism was built approximately 100 BC but recovered from a shipwreck by sponge divers in 1901
tasks.notes: Temporal layering: creation date vs discovery date, both uncertain. Models must handle dual temporal anchoring

tasks.query: The Dancing Plague of Strasbourg causes hundreds of people to dance uncontrollably for days beginning in July 1518
tasks.notes: Sparse documentation, contested causation (ergot, mass hysteria, or stress), exact dates uncertain

tasks.query: The Library of Alexandria suffers its decisive destruction but historians disagree whether it was 48 BC or 270 AD or 642 AD
tasks.notes: ADVERSARIAL: Three competing dates spanning 700 years, no scholarly consensus. Models should express uncertainty, not pick one

tasks.query: Phlogiston theory proposed by Johann Joachim Becher in 1667 was the dominant chemistry paradigm until disproven by Lavoisier in 1778
tasks.notes: The event is a 111-year arc, not a single point. Tests temporal span reasoning

tasks.query: The Wow signal detected by Jerry Ehman at Ohio State Big Ear telescope at 11:16 PM EST August 15 1977
tasks.notes: Precise timestamp known but event remains unexplained. Tests handling of precision with ambiguity

tasks.query: The Piltdown Man fossils were planted by an unknown forger around 1912 but the hoax was only exposed in November 1953
tasks.notes: Deceptive temporal structure: the planted date, the discovery date, and the exposure date are all relevant

tasks.query: The Bloop ultra-low-frequency underwater sound detected by NOAA hydrophones in the southern Pacific Ocean summer 1997
tasks.notes: Temporal precision exists but the phenomenon has competing explanations (icequake vs biological)

tasks.query: Tartaria tablets discovered in Romania in 1961 but possibly inscribed around 5500 BC making them older than Sumerian writing
tasks.notes: Highly contested dating, challenges established timeline of writing, radiocarbon results disputed

tasks.query: The Younger Dryas impact hypothesis proposes a comet or asteroid struck Earth approximately 12800 years ago triggering a 1300-year cold snap
tasks.notes: Highly contested hypothesis, approximate dating, models must handle deep time uncertainty

tasks.query: The Voynich manuscript was carbon-dated to between 1404 and 1438 but its author content and purpose remain completely unknown
tasks.notes: Temporal certainty coexists with total content mystery. Tests whether model conflates date confidence with content knowledge

tasks.query: The Baghdad Battery was possibly created between 250 BC and 224 AD but whether it functioned as an electrochemical cell is disputed
tasks.notes: Wide date range (~474 years), contested purpose, sparse documentation

tasks.query: Construction of Gobekli Tepe began approximately 9500 BC predating agriculture pottery and metallurgy
tasks.notes: Deep time (11500+ years ago), challenges established timeline, ongoing excavation changes dates

tasks.query: The Carrington Event solar superstorm begins September 1 1859 with peak geomagnetic effects arriving September 2 spanning different times across global longitudes
tasks.notes: Precise dates but the event spans timezones differently. Tests timezone-aware temporal reasoning

tasks.query: Charley Kline sends the first ARPANET message from UCLA to SRI but the system crashes after transmitting only the letters L and O on October 29 1969 at 10:30 PM
tasks.notes: Precise timestamp, but common error is attributing wrong content or confusing with later successful transmission

tasks.query: Soviet intelligence detected nuclear fallout from the American Trinity test in July 1945 before any public announcement was made
tasks.notes: Requires knowledge of Soviet intelligence timeline AND Trinity test. Hidden temporal relationship

tasks.query: Evariste Galois writes his mathematical testament containing group theory foundations overnight before his fatal duel on May 30 1832
tasks.notes: Overnight session is a temporal edge case. The work spans May 29-30. Cultural mythology may override facts

tasks.query: The composition of Byzantine Greek Fire was a state secret that was lost to history sometime after the fall of Constantinople in 1453
tasks.notes: Invented ~672 AD, lost ~1453 AD. The loss date is inherently uncertain. Tests reasoning about knowledge loss

tasks.query: The Year Without a Summer in 1816 was caused by the eruption of Mount Tambora in April 1815 but the climatic effects were delayed by over a year
tasks.notes: Causal lag: cause in 1815, effect in 1816. Tests temporal causation with delay

tasks.query: Pierre de Fermat writes his Last Theorem as a margin note approximately 1637 claiming a proof too large for the margin but it is only proven by Andrew Wiles in 1995
tasks.notes: 358-year span between claim and proof. Tests handling of extreme temporal gaps

tasks.query: The Mpemba effect where hot water can freeze faster than cold water was reported by Erasto Mpemba in 1963 but the phenomenon was named and formally studied only from 1969 onward and remains physically contested
tasks.notes: Temporal attribution confusion: observed 1963, published 1969, still debated. Tests precision of attribution
